1.4

QUALITY ASSURANCE

In each standard referred to herein, consider the advisory provisions to
be mandatory, as though the word "shall" or "must" has been substituted
for "should" wherever it appears. Interpret references in these standards
to "authority having jurisdiction," or words of similar meaning, to mean
Contracting Officer.
1.4.1
1.4.1.1

Installation Drawings
Overall System Drawing

Submit installation shop drawing for the overall lightning protection
system. Include on the drawings the physical layout of the equipment
(plan view and elevations), mounting details, relationship to other parts
of the work, and wiring diagrams.

SECTION 26 41 00

Page 2

Niagara Falls ARS, B917 Roof Repair

1.4.1.2

NIAGARAROOF

Major Components

Submit detail drawings for each major component including manufacturer's
descriptive and technical literature, catalog cuts, and installation
instructions.
1.4.2

Component UL Listed and Labeled

Submit proof of compliance that components are UL Listed and Labeled.
Listing alone in UL Electrical Construction, which is the UL Electrical
Construction Directory, is not acceptable evidence. In lieu of Listed and
Labeled, submit written certificate from an approved, nationally
recognized testing organization equipped to perform such services, stating
that items have been tested and conform to requirements and testing
methods of Underwriters Laboratories.
1.4.3

Lightning Protection and Grounding System Test Plan

Provide a lightning protection and grounding system test plan. Detail
both the visual inspection and electrical testing of the system and
components in the test plan. Identify (number) the system test
points/locations along with a listing or description of the item to be
tested and the type of test to be conducted. As a minimum, include a
sketch of the facility and surrounding lightning protection system as part
of the specific test plan for each structure. Include the requirements
specified in paragraph, "Testing of Integral Lightning Protection System"
in the test plan.
1.4.4

Lightning Protection System Inspection Certificate

Provide certification from a commercial third-party inspection company
whose sole work is lightning protection, stating that the lightning
protection system complies with NFPA 780. Third party inspection company
cannot be the system installer or the system designer. Alternatively,
provide a UL Lightning Protection Inspection Master Label Certificate for
each facility indicating compliance to NFPA 780.
1.5

SITE CONDITIONS

Confirm all details of work, verify all dimensions in field, and advise
Contracting Officer of any discrepancy before performing work. Obtain
prior approval of Contracting Officer before changing the design.
PART 2
2.1

PRODUCTS
MATERIALS

Do not use a combination of materials that forms an electrolytic couple of
such nature that corrosion is accelerated in the presence of moisture
unless moisture is permanently excluded from the junction of such metals.
Where unusual conditions exist which would cause corrosion of conductors,
provide conductors with protective coatings, such as tin or lead, or
oversize conductors. Where a mechanical hazard is involved, increase
conductor size to compensate for the hazard or protect conductors. When
metallic conduit or tubing is provided, electrically bond conductor to
conduit or tubing at the upper and lower ends by clamp type connectors or
welds (including exothermic). All lightning protection components, such
as bonding plates, air terminals, air terminal supports and braces,
chimney bands, clips, connector fittings, and fasteners are to comply with
SECTION 26 41 00

Page 3

Niagara Falls ARS, B917 Roof Repair

NIAGARAROOF

the requirements of UL 96 classes as applicable.
2.1.1

Main and Bonding Conductors

NFPA 780 and UL 96 Class I, Class II, or Class II modified materials as
applicable.
2.1.2

Copper Only

Provide copper conductors, except where aluminum conductors are required
for connection to aluminum equipment.
2.2

COMPONENTS

2.2.1

Air Terminals

Provide solid air terminals with a blunt tip. Tubular air terminals are
not permitted. Support air terminals more than 24 inches in length by
suitable brace, supported at not less than one-half the height of the
terminal.
2.2.2

Connections and Terminations

Provide connectors for splicing conductors that conform to UL 96, class as
applicable. Conductor connections can be made by clamps or welds
(including exothermic). Provide style and size connectors required for
the installation.
2.2.3

Connector Fittings

Provide connector fittings for "end-to-end", "Tee", or "Y" splices that
conform to NFPA 780 and UL 96.
PART 3
3.1

EXECUTION
INTEGRAL SYSTEM

Provide a lightning protection system that meets the requirements of
NFPA 780. Lightning protection system consists of air terminals, roof
conductors, down conductors, ground connections. Bond secondary
conductors with grounded metallic parts within the building. Make
interconnections within side-flash distances at or below the level of the
grounded metallic parts.
3.1.1

Roof-Mounted Components

Coordinate with the roofing manufacturer and provide certification that
the roof manufacturer's warranty is not violated by the installation
methods for air terminals and roof conductors.
3.1.1.1

Air Terminals

Use adhesive shoes with adhesive approved by the roof manufacturer when
installing air terminals on "rubber" (EPDM) type roofs. In areas of snow
or constant wind, ensure that a section of roofing material (minimum
dimensional area of 1 square foot) is first glued to the roof and then the
air terminal is glued to it unless the roof manufacturer recommends
another solution. Use a standing seam base for installation of air
terminals on a standing seam metal roof that does not produce any roof
SECTION 26 41 00

Page 4

Niagara Falls ARS, B917 Roof Repair

NIAGARAROOF

penetrations.
3.1.1.2

Roof Conductors

Use adhesive shoes with adhesive approved by the roof manufacturer when
installing roof conductors on "rubber" (EPDM) type roofs. Use a standing
seam base for installation of roof conductors on a standing seam metal
roof that does not produce any roof penetrations.
3.1.2

Down Conductors

Protect exposed down conductors from physical damage as required by
NFPA 780. Use Schedule 80 PVC to protect down conductors. Paint the
Schedule 80 PVC to match the surrounding surface with paint that is
approved for use on PVC.
3.1.3

Ground Connections

Attach each down conductor to ground rods by welding (including
exothermic), brazing, or compression. All connections to ground rods
below ground level must be by exothermic weld connection or with a high
compression connection using a hydraulic or electric compression tool to
provide the correct circumferential pressure. Accessible connections
above ground level and in test wells can be accomplished by mechanical
clamping.
3.2
3.2.1

APPLICATIONS
Nonmetallic Exterior Walls with Metallic Roof

Bond metal roof sections together which are insulated from each other so
that they are electrically continuous, having a surface contact of at least
3 square inches.
3.3
3.3.1

FIELD QUALITY CONTROL
Lightning Protection and Grounding System Test

Test the lightning protection and grounding system to ensure continuity is
not in excess of 1 ohm and that resistance to ground is not in excess of 25
ohms. Provide documentation for the measured values at each test point.
Test the ground rod for resistance to ground before making connections to
the rod. Tie the grounding system together and test for resistance to
ground. Make resistance measurements in dry weather, not earlier than 48
hours after rainfall. Include in the written report: locations of test
points, measured values for continuity and ground resistances, and soil
conditions at the time that measurements were made. Submit results of
each test to the Contracting Officer.
-- End of Section --

DEPARTMENT OF THE AIR FORCE

23 Aug 2024
ADDENDUM 1 to
Repair B-917 Roof, RVKQ 20-0773
1. Delete the following requirement from the project scope:
Demolish pop-up skylight cupola and install infill steel beams, decking and standing
seam metal roofing in place of the pop-up cupola. Reference marked up attached
Sheet S-101, Detail A3 on Sheet S-102, Sheet AD101, AE101, and Detail D5 on
Sheet AE501.
2. Existing pop-up cupola is to remain in place. Install new roofing and flashing around
existing pop-up cupola as necessary to form a watertight seal.
3. The requirement to demolish translucent panel system and infill with applicable
beams, decking, and roofing elsewhere on the roof is to remain in the project.

E

3.

C

B

PHASING:
a.
THE PROJECT SITE IS ADJACENT TO AN AIRFIELD PARKING APRON THE AIRFIELD MUST REMAIN SECURE AT ALL
TIMES AND NO CONTRACTOR PERSONNEL OR EQUIPMENT SHALL ENTER THE AIRFIELD WITHOUT AND ESCORT
FROM THE BASE.
PHASE 1: DURING PHASE 1, THERE WILL BE NO ACTIVE AIRCRAFT ON THE APRON OR IN THE HANGAR BAY.
THE CONTRACTOR SHALL INSTALL 32” HIGH TRAFFIC BARRIERS AT THE LOCATION SHOWN ON SHEET
G-002 PRIOR TO COMMENCING WORK FROM THE APRON AND INSIDE THE HANGAR BAY. GAPS BETWEEN
BARRIERS SHALL NOT EXCEED FIVE (5) FEET AND EACH BARRIER SHALL HAVE A FLASHING RED HAZARD
LIGHT.
PHASE I SHALL BE LIMITED TO 30 CALENDAR DAYS. THE CONTRACTOR SHALL BE PERMITTED TO WORK
FROM THE APRON IN FRONT OF THE HANGAR DOOR TO REMOVE THE CUPOLA AND COMPLETE THE
CLOSURE OF THIS SPACE. THE CONTRACTOR SHALL HAVE ACCESS TO THE HANGAR INTERIOR TO
COMPLETE ALL WORK REQUIRED ON THE INTERIOR ROOF PANEL, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO
PATCHING AND PAINTING, DISCONNECTION AND RECONNECTION OF ROOFTOP EQUIPMENT TO REMAIN,
REPLACEMENT OF TRANSLUCENT PANELS WITH ROOFING PANELS AND OTHER TASKS THAT REQUIRE THE
ROOF TO BE OPEN OR THAT MUST BE COMPLETED FROM THE INTERIOR OF THE HANGAR.
PHASE 2:
THERE MAY BE ACTIVE AIRCRAFT UNDERGOING MAINTENANCE IN THE HANGAR AT THE DISCRETION OF THE
GOVERNMENT. THE CONTRACTOR SHALL REMOVE THE BARRIERS PLACED ON THE APRON DURING PHASE 1
AND INSTALL THE PHASE 2 BARRIERS AS SHOWN ON SHEET G-002. A CONTINUOUS COMPOST FILTER LOG
WILL BE INSTALLED ON THE OUTER PERIMETER OF THE PHASE 2 BARRIERS TO PREVENT SEDIMENT
MIGRATION INTO THE EXISTING DRAINAGE SWALE BEFORE COMMENCING PHASE 2.
DURING PHASE 2, THE CONTRACTOR SHALL STOP WORK AND SECURE LOOSE MATERIALS AT ANY TIME
WHEN AN AIRCRAFT IS UNDER TOW INTO OR OUT OF THE HANGAR. THE CONTRACTOR SHALL MAINTAIN A
CLEAR PATHWAY FOR THE AIRCRAFT TUG TO ENTER THE NOSE DOCK. TOWING TIME IS ANTICIPATED TO BE
LESS THAN ONE HOUR FOR EACH OCCURRENCE. THE HANGAR DOOR AND APRON MUST REMAIN FULLY
ACCESSIBLE FOR THE ENTRANCE AND EXIT OF AIRCRAFT FROM THE HANGAR.
DURING THIS TIME THE CONTRACTOR SHALL HAVE ACCESS TO THE EXTERIOR OF THE ROOF TO COMPLETE
THE INSTALLATION OF NEW ROOFING MATERIALS, INSTALLATION OF FLASHINGS, GUTTERS AND
DOWNSPOUTS , INSTALLATION OF SNOW GUARDS, LIGHTNING PROTECTION SYSTEM AND THE ROOF TOP
FALL PROTECTION SYSTEM AND ANY OTHER TASKS THAT CAN BE COMPLETED FROM THE EXTERIOR OF THE
FACILITY.
UPON THE COMPLETION AND ACCEPTANCE OF THE ROOF, THE CONTRACTOR SHALL REMOVE ALL
MATERIALS, EQUIPMENT AND BARRIERS FROM THE SITE AND RESTORE ALL DISTURBED SURFACES.

.
b.

A
c.

DURING ALL PHASES OF OPERATION, THE CONTRACTORS FIRST RESPONSIBILITY MUST BE SAFETY. THUS, THE
CONTRACTOR MUST BE REQUIRED TO KEEP THE ROADWAYS, TAXIWAYS, AND APRONS FREE OF ALL
EQUIPMENT, DEBRIS AND WASTE PRODUCTS EXCEPT FOR THOSE EXCLUSIVELY REQUIRED IN THE
CONTRACTOR'S WORK AREA. AS AN ADDED MEASURE OF SAFETY, THE CONTRACTOR MUST HAVE AT LEAST
ONE VACUUM SWEEPER PER EACH CREW WHILE CONSTRUCTION OPERATION WORK IS IN PROGRESS. THE
CONTRACTOR MUST MAINTAIN ACCESS TO VACUUM SWEEPERS, WATERING TRUCKS AND OTHER EQUIPMENT
NECESSARY TO CONTROL DUST AT ALL TIMES ON THE PROJECT. DUST CONTROL MUST BE STRICTLY
MONITORED DUE TO ITS IMPACT ON AIRCRAFT SAFETY. THE CONTRACTOR IS CAUTIONED THAT THEY CAN BE
DIRECTED TO VACATE A WORK AREA UPON 30 MINUTES' NOTICE; THEREFORE, IT IS IMPERATIVE THAT THE WORK
AREA BE MAINTAINED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE ABOVE. FAILURE TO PROPERLY CONTROL DUST OR TO
RESPOND TO ANY REQUEST TO DO SO WILL RESULT IN CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES BEING STOPPED AT NO
ADDITIONAL COST TO THE GOVERNMENT.
CONSTRUCTION SAFETY DRAWINGS - REFER TO PLANS FOR SAFETY PROCEDURES THAT INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT
LIMITED TO, TRAFFIC CONTROL, SCHEDULING CLOSURES FOR CONSTRUCTION, HAUL ROUTES, AND REQUIRED
HAZARD MARKING AND LIGHTING.

7

8

9

5.

PROTECTION OF NAVAIDS:
BEFORE COMMENCING CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITY, PARKING VEHICLES, OR STORING CONSTRUCTION EQUIPMENT AND
MATERIALS NEAR A NAVAID, THE CONTRACTOR MUST COORDINATE WITH AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT AND BASE CES WHO
WILL COORDINATE TO EVALUATE THE EFFECT OF CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITY, AND DETERMINE THE REQUIRED
DISTANCES AND DIRECTION FROM THE NAVAID. CONTRACTOR PERSONNEL MUST BE AWARE OF CRITICAL AREA
LOCATIONS AND REMAIN CLEAR OF THE AREAS DURING LOW VISIBILITY. NO MATERIAL OR CONSTRUCTION WILL BE
STOCKPILED OR STORED NEAR ANY NAVAIDS.
7.

6.

CONTRACTOR ACCESS
a.
LOCATION OF STOCKPILED CONSTRUCTION MATERIALS - THE CONTRACTOR MAY SET UP A STAGING AREA
OUTSIDE OF THE AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS AREA (AOA) AS DIRECTED BY THE CONTRACTING OFFICER. THIS AREA
MUST BE USED FOR MATERIAL STOCKPILING AND LAYDOWN; COORDINATE ANY TRAILER REQUIREMENT WITH
THE COR AFTER AWARD. THE EXACT LIMITS OF THE CONTRACTOR'S STAGING AREA MUST BE ESTABLISHED BY
THE CONTRACTOR WITH THE APPROVAL OF THE COR IN THE AREA SHOWN. THE CONSTRUCTION SITE MUST BE
ENCLOSED BY TEMPORARY SECURITY FENCING AS DEFINED BY THE COR.
b.
VEHICLE AND PEDESTRIAN OPERATIONS - THE CONTRACTOR MUST REFER TO SHEETS G-002 FOR STAGING
AREA LOCATION, CONSTRUCTION ACCESS GATE, AND VEHICLE ROUTES TO AND FROM THE CONSTRUCTION SITE.
i.
CONSTRUCTION SITE PARKING - CONTRACTOR EMPLOYEE PERSONAL VEHICLES MAY NOT BE PARKED OR
DRIVEN ON BASE WITHOUT APPROVAL FROM THE COR AND NEVER ON THE FLIGHT LINE. THE CONTRACTOR
MUST DIRECT EMPLOYEES TO THE DESIGNATED VEHICLE PARKING AREA LOCATED OUTSIDE THE AIRFIELD.
ii.
CONSTRUCTION EQUIPMENT PARKING - CONTRACTOR VEHICLES AND EQUIPMENT ARE ALLOWED INSIDE OF
THE PROJECT WORK AREA WITHIN THE BASE WHERE APPROVED BY THE COR AND AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT.
NO NON-ACTIVE CONTRACTOR EQUIPMENT IS PERMITTED WITHIN AN ACTIVE RUNWAY PRIMARY SURFACE
(1,000 FT FROM CENTERLINE), AN ACTIVE RUNWAY CLEARANCE ZONE (1,500 FT FROM CENTERLINE), OR THE
TAXIWAY CLEARANCE AREA (200 FT FROM THE CENTERLINE).
iii.
ACCESS AND HAUL ROUTES - THE CONTRACTOR'S ACCESS ROUTES TO THE PROJECT SITES MUST BE VIA
THE COMMERCIAL GATE AS SHOWN IN THE PLANS, UNLESS OTHERWISE COORDINATED WITH THE COR.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST REPAIR ANY AND ALL HAUL ROUTES DAMAGED BY CONSTRUCTION/HAULING
ACTIVITIES AT NO ADDITIONAL COST TO THE GOVERNMENT. USE OF UNAUTHORIZED HAUL ROUTES MUST
NOT BE PERMITTED. ROADS USED BY THE CONTRACTOR FOR ACCESS OR HAULING MUST BE KEPT CLEAN
AND ACCESSIBLE TO ALL OTHER BASE AND AIRPORT TRAFFIC FOR THE ENTIRE DURATION OF THE
PROJECT.
WHEN WORKING INSIDE THE AIRFIELD PERIMETER FENCE, THE CONTRACTOR MUST BE RESPONSIBLE
FOR OBTAINING PRIOR APPROVAL FROM AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT AND USE BASE PROVIDED ESCORTS.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE AN OPERATOR WITH A VACUUM SWEEPER TO KEEP THE AREA FREE
FROM DUST AND DEBRIS.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST COORDINATE WITH EXISTING AND FUTURE CONTRACTORS WORKING IN THE
AREA AND AT ALL TIMES WILL COORDINATE EFFORTS TO MAINTAIN THE NECESSARY CONSTRUCTION
ACCESS ROUTES, AND TO ASSURE THAT ALL CONTRACTS CONTINUE ON A TIMELY BASIS.
iv. CONTRACTOR VEHICLE MARKING AND LIGHTING - THE SITE WILL HAVE A CONSTRUCTION AREA FREE ZONE.
NO CONTRACTOR VEHICLES MUST ENTER THE AIRFIELD WITHOUT AN ESCORT. IF REQUIRED, THE
CONTRACTOR MUST COORDINATE WITH CONTRACTING OFFICER 14-DAYS IN ADVANCE. MARKING AND
LIGHTING IS NOT REQUIRED IN THE CONSTRUCTION AREA FREE ZONE. IF AIRFIELD ACCESS IS REQUIRED,
MARKING AND LIGHTING OF VEHICLES MUST BE IN ACCORDANCE WITH FAA AC 150/5210-5D, PAINTING,
MARKING AND LIGHTING OF VEHICLES USED ON AN AIRPORT.
v.
PROPER VEHICLE OPERATIONS - NO PERSON MUST OPERATE MOTORIZED VEHICLES OR EQUIPMENT OF
ANY KIND ON THE AIRPORT UNLESS IN POSSESSION OF VALID OPERATOR'S LICENSE FOR THE TYPE OF
VEHICLE BEING OPERATED.
NO PERSON MUST OPERATE A MOTOR VEHICLE OR OTHER MOTORIZED EQUIPMENT OF ANY KIND ON
THE AIRPORT IN A RECKLESS OR NEGLIGENT MANNER OR WITHOUT CAUTION, OR IN ANY MANNER THAT
ENDANGERS OR IS LIKELY TO ENDANGER PERSONS OR PROPERTY, OR IN EXCESS OF THE POSTED SPEED
LIMIT. NO PERSON UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF ALCOHOL OR DRUGS MUST OPERATE A MOTOR VEHICLE ON
THE AIRPORT.
ALL VEHICLES AND PEDESTRIANS MUST GIVE RIGHT-OF-WAY TO AIRCRAFT TRAFFIC, NO
EXCEPTIONS. ALL GROUND VEHICLES MUST PASS TO THE REAR OF TAXIING AIRCRAFT AFTER
ACCOUNTING FOR POSSIBLE JET BLAST EFFECTS.
CONTRACTOR WILL NOT BE ALLOWED TO OPERATE MOTOR VEHICLES OUTSIDE OF THE DESIGNATED
WORK AREAS AS IDENTIFIED BY BARRICADES INCLUSIVE OF THE APPROVED CONTRACTOR HAUL ROUTE.
NO CROSSING OF ACTIVE TAXIWAYS IS PERMITTED WITH THE EXCEPTION OF A CROSSING THAT INCLUDES
FLAGMAN OR ESCORT VEHICLE THAT HAS RADIO CONTACT WITH THE ATCT. IN THE EVENT THAT THE
CONTRACTOR MUST ACCESS THE ACTIVE FLIGHTLINE, THE CONTRACTOR MUST BE ESCORTED BY THE COR
OR A FLIGHTLINE ESCORT. ABSOLUTELY NO CROSSING OF ACTIVE RUNWAYS OR INCURSIONS INTO ACTIVE
RUNWAY SAFETY AREAS IS PERMITTED.
IN THE EVENT OF LOSS OF COMMUNICATION BETWEEN THE FAA ATCT AND COR AND/OR FLIGHTLINE
ESCORT, AIRFIELD OPERATIONS MUST BE NOTIFIED AND ALL ACTIVE FLIGHTLINE MOVEMENTS ARE TO BE
SUSPENDED UNTIL THE ISSUE IS RECTIFIED. DURING EMERGENCY SITUATIONS, THE CONTRACTOR MUST
IMMEDIATELY NOTIFY AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT AND THE COR. ALL WORK WILL BE HALTED UNTIL THE
EMERGENCY HAS BEEN RESOLVED.
vi. DRIVER TRAINING - ALL DRIVERS OPERATING VEHICLES WITHIN THE FLIGHTLINE MUST OBTAIN APPROVAL
FROM AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT IN ADDITION TO BASE BADGING AND FLIGHTLINE BADGING. ALL VEHICLES
THAT HAVE AN OPERATIONAL NEED TO ACCESS BASE OR FLIGHTLINE ARE REQUIRED TO HAVE BASE AND
FLIGHTLINE VEHICLE PERMIT STICKERS FOR THE AREA IN WHICH THAT VEHICLE WILL OPERATE.
NIAGARA FALLS ARS WILL REQUIRE AIRPORT DRIVER SAFETY TRAINING AND RAMP CERTIFICATION
FOR ALL PERSONNEL REQUIRED TO OPERATE A MOTOR VEHICLE ON THE FLIGHTLINE. THIS CAN BE
OBTAINED BY COMPLETING AN AIRPORT DRIVER SAFETY TRAINING COURSE ADMINISTERED BY THE
AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT DIVISION. THESE DRIVERS MUST ONLY OPERATE VEHICLES IN APPROVED AREAS
FOR THE PROJECT. DRIVERS REQUIRING ACCESS TO DRIVE ON THE FLIGHTLINE MUST COMPLETE
ADDITIONAL TRAINING PROVIDED BY AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT. CONTRACTOR MUST CONTACT AIRFIELD
MANAGEMENT TO SCHEDULE THE TRAINING SESSION.

8.

FOREIGN OBJECT DEBRIS (FOD) MANAGEMENT:
a.
FOD AT AIRPORTS INCLUDES ANY LOOSE OBJECT(S) FOUND THAT CAN DAMAGE AIRCRAFT, EQUIPMENT, OR
AIRPORT PERSONNEL. ON CONSTRUCTION SITES, FOD TYPICALLY IS COMPRISED OF LOOSE GRAVEL, BLOWING
SAND, WIRE BRISTLES FROM SWEEPER HEADS, FOOD WRAPPERS, MATERIAL PACKAGING, AMONG OTHER
DEBRIS. THE PRESENCE OF FOD IN THE AMA POSES A SIGNIFICANT THREAT TO THE SAFETY OF AIR TRAVEL.
FOD HAS THE POTENTIAL TO DAMAGE AIRCRAFT DURING CRITICAL PHASES OF FLIGHT, WHICH CAN LEAD TO
CATASTROPHIC LOSS OF LIFE AND AIRCRAFT, AND AT THE VERY LEAST INCREASED MAINTENANCE AND
OPERATING COSTS. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PERFORM CONSTRUCTION IN A MANNER THAT, AT THE END OF
THE CLOSURE PERIOD, THE SAFETY AREAS AND AIRFIELD PAVEMENT ARE CLEAN AND IN A CONDITION SUITABLE
FOR AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT TO REOPEN THE PAVEMENT SECTION TO AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS.
b.
CONTRACTOR IS RESPONSIBLE FOR ALL FOD ON OR ORIGINATING FROM WORK AREA OR HAUL ROUTES.
c.
CONTRACTOR MUST BE HELD RESPONSIBLE TO PROVIDE A FOD MANAGEMENT PREVENTION PLAN THAT SHOULD
ADDRESS THE FOLLOWING:
i.
PROPER STORAGE, SHIPPING AND HANDLING OF MATERIAL COMPONENTS AND EQUIPMENT.
ii.
COMPONENTS AND EQUIPMENT TECHNIQUES TO CONTROL DEBRIS.
iii.
HOUSEKEEPING
iv. ACCOUNTABILITY/CONTROL OF TOOLS AND HARDWARE.
v.
CONTROL OF PERSONAL ITEMS, EQUIPMENT AND CONSUMABLES.
vi. QUALITY WORKMANSHIP (“CLEAN AS YOU GO” INSPECTIONS).
vii. HOW TO REPORT FOD INCIDENTS OR POTENTIAL INCIDENTS.
d.
CONTRACTOR MUST INSTRUCT PERSONNEL ABOUT THE IMPORTANCE OF MANAGING THEIR WASTE MATERIALS
AND CONDUCT DAILY INSPECTION OF THE WORK SITE TO ENSURE THAT LOOSE MATERIALS ARE SECURED.
e.
CONTRACTOR OPERATIONS ON OR NEAR AIRCRAFT OPERATING SURFACES MUST BE KEPT CLEAN OF LOOSE
DEBRIS. DIRT AND DEBRIS CREATED BY CONTRACTOR OPERATIONS MUST BE PROMPTLY REMOVED AND THE
SURFACE CLEANED.

9.

HAZARDOUS MATERIAL (HAZMAT) MANAGEMENT:
a.
CONTRACTOR OPERATING ANY MECHANICAL EQUIPMENT ON THE AIRPORT MUST BE PREPARED TO IMMEDIATELY
SUSPEND WORK TO MITIGATE AND CLEAN SPILLS FROM FUEL, HYDRAULIC FLUID, OR OTHER CHEMICAL SPILLS.
b.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST NOTIFY AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT AS SOON AS A SPILL OCCURS. WASHING CHEMICALS
DOWN A DRAIN OR INTO GRASS WITH WATER IS NEVER PERMITTED. CONTRACTOR MUST BE FAMILIAR WITH
HAZMAT PROCEDURES PRIOR TO ISSUANCE OF THE NOTICE TO PROCEED (NTP) INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO
FUEL STORAGE, SPILL PROCEDURES, AND MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEETS (MSDS)
c.
CONTRACTOR MUST MAINTAIN A COMPLETE FILE OF MSDS SHEETS ON SITE AS REQUIRED. COPIES OF MSDS
SHEETS MUST BE PROVIDED TO THE COR PRIOR TO BRINGING MATERIALS ONTO AIRPORT PROPERTY.
CONTRACTOR MUST MAINTAIN CLEANING AND CONTAINMENT EQUIPMENT ON SITE APPROPRIATE FOR ANY
HAZARDOUS MATERIALS USED DURING CONSTRUCTION.

10.

NOTIFICATION OF CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES:
THE FOLLOWING IS INFORMATION AND PROCEDURES FOR IMMEDIATE NOTIFICATION OF AIRPORT USERS AND THE FAA
OF ANY CONDITIONS ADVERSELY AFFECTING THE OPERATIONAL SAFETY OF THE AIRPORT. CONTRACTOR MUST
COORDINATE ALL NOTIFICATIONS THROUGH THE COR.
a.
RESPONSIBLE REPRESENTATIVES/POINT OF CONTACTS: THE COR WILL PROVIDE THE CONTRACTOR WITH A
REPRESENTATIVE CONTACT LIST DURING THE PRE-CONSTRUCTION CONFERENCE, TO INCLUDE EMERGENCY
RESPONSE NUMBERS. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE A LIST OF RESPONSIBLE REPRESENTATIVES AND
THEIR PHONE NUMBERS TO THE COR. LIST MUST BE UPDATED AT EACH CONSTRUCTION PROGRESS MEETING AS
REQUIRED.
b.
NOTICES TO AIRMEN (NOTAM) - ONLY THE AIRPORT MAY INITIATE OR CANCEL NOTAMS ON AIRPORT CONDITIONS,
AND IS THE ONLY ENTITY THAT CAN CLOSE OR OPEN TAXIWAYS OR RUNWAYS. CONTRACTOR MUST
COMMUNICATE WITH THE COR ABOUT CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES A MINIMUM OF 48-HOURS IN ADVANCE. THE
COR WILL NOTIFY AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT SO THAT NOTAMS CAN BE ISSUED IN A TIMELY MANNER. THE COR
MUST KEEP AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT INFORMED OF THE STATUS OF THE CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITY SO THAT
NOTAM STATUS CAN BE MAINTAINED.

DESCRIPTION

AREAS AND OPERATIONS AFFECTED BY CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES:
a.
RUNWAYS AND TAXIWAYS WILL REMAIN IN USE BY AIRCRAFT TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT POSSIBLE WITHOUT
COMPROMISING SAFETY. PRE CONSTRUCTION MEETINGS WITH BASE CONS AND CES, AND AIRFIELD
MANAGEMENT WILL ADDRESS OPERATIONAL AFFECTS.
b.
IDENTIFICATION OF AFFECTED AREAS - THIS PROJECT WILL NOT LIMIT AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS ON THE TAXIWAY.
c.
ANY CONSTRUCTION WITHIN 10,000 FT OF A RUNWAY CENTERLINE REQUIRES INSTALLATION COMMANDER
APPROVAL THROUGH A TEMPORARY AIRFIELD CONSTRUCTION WAIVER.
d.
ANY CONSTRUCTION WITHIN 200 FT OF A TAXIWAY CENTERLINE REQUIRES INSTALLATION COMMANDER
APPROVAL THROUGH A TEMPORARY AIRFIELD CONSTRUCTION WAIVER. A WAIVER IS NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS
PROJECT.
e.
ANY CONSTRUCTION WITHIN THE 3,000 FT x 3,000 FT CLEAR ZONE BEYOND THE END OF THE RUNWAY REQUIRES
INSTALLATION COMMANDER APPROVAL THROUGH A TEMPORARY AIRFIELD CONSTRUCTION WAIVER. A WAIVER
IS NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS PROJECT.
f.
MITIGATION OF EFFECTS - THIS PROJECT IS WITHIN AN AREA OF CONTINUOUS LARGE JET AIRCRAFT
OPERATIONS. THE CONTRACTOR MUST TAKE ALL PRECAUTIONS NECESSARY TO PROTECT ITS' EMPLOYEES,
EQUIPMENT, AND WORK IN PROGRESS FROM AIRCRAFT NOISE AND JET ENGINE BLAST.

DATE

vii.

4.

SITUATIONAL AWARENESS - CONTRACTOR OPERATIONS WITHIN THE BASE OR FLIGHTLINE ARE LIMITED TO
THE WORK AREAS SHOWN ON THE PLANS. A VISUAL DELINEATION WILL BE PROVIDED BY THE
CONTRACTOR BETWEEN THE WORK AREAS AND THE RUNWAY SAFETY AREA AND TAXIWAY OBJECT FREE
AREA WHEN DIRECTLY ADJACENT TO ONE ANOTHER. THE PLANS SHOW LOCATIONS OF WORK AREA
BOUNDARIES, WHICH GENERALLY FOLLOW OBJECT FREE AREAS OF ADJACENT OPEN PAVEMENT.
CONSTRUCTION VEHICLES AND PERSONNEL MUST NOT CROSS BARRICADES OR OTHER VISUAL
IDENTIFIERS AT ANY TIME WITHOUT A PROPER ESCORT OR APPROVAL FROM GROUND CONTROL.
DRIVERS MUST BE ESPECIALLY COGNIZANT AND AWARE WHILE DRIVING ON THE AIRPORT. VEHICLE
DRIVERS MUST CONFIRM BY PERSONAL OBSERVATION THAT NO AIRCRAFT IS APPROACHING THEIR
POSITION (EITHER IN THE AIR OR ON THE GROUND) WHEN GIVEN CLEARANCE TO CROSS A TAXIWAY, OR
ANY OTHER AREA OPEN TO AIRSIDE OPERATIONS.
viii. RADIO COMMUNICATIONS - WHEN OPERATING VEHICLES ON OR NEAR OPEN RUNWAYS OR TAXIWAYS, ALL
PARTIES MUST UNDERSTAND THE CRITICAL IMPORTANCE OF MAINTAINING RADIO CONTACT, AS DIRECTED
BY AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT DURING DRIVER TRAINING. THE TRAINING PROVIDED BY AIRFIELD
MANAGEMENT ENCOMPASSES SITUATIONAL AWARENESS, FAMILIARIZATION, PROPER RADIO
PHRASEOLOGY, AND FREQUENCIES. AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT MAY DESIGNATE THE COR, AND IN ADDITION
TO, AN ESCORT OR CROSSING GUARD TO COMMUNICATE WITH THE FAA ATCT TO PROVIDE THE
CONTRACTOR AND THEIR PERSONNEL WITH ESCORTS OR CROSSING POINTS ON THE FLIGHTLINE. THE
INSPECTOR, FLIGHTLINE ESCORT, AND CROSSING GUARD MUST COORDINATE WITH AIRFIELD
MANAGEMENT FOR APPROPRIATE FREQUENCIES TO BE USED.
ix.
SECURITY - ALL CONTRACTOR PERSONNEL AND SUBCONTRACTORS WORKING WITHIN THE AIRFIELD MUST
HAVE AN NIAGARA FALLS ARS ESCORT. ALL PERSONNEL DRIVING ON THE FLIGHTLINE ARE REQUIRED TO
PASS THE NECESSARY EXAMS AND DRIVER'S TEST AS OUTLINED IN THE CONTRACT SPECIFICATIONS. THE
BASE INCLUDES ALL AREAS WITHIN THE AIRFIELD SECURITY FENCE. THE FLIGHTLINE INCLUDES AREAS IN
OR WITHIN CLOSE PROXIMITY TO AIRCRAFT MOVEMENT AREAS.
WILDLIFE MANAGEMENT:
CONTRACTOR MUST CAREFULLY CONTROL AND CONTINUOUSLY REMOVE WASTE OR LOOSE MATERIALS THAT MIGHT
ATTRACT WILDLIFE. THE CONTRACTOR MUST BE AWARE OF AND AVOID CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES THAT CAN CREATE
WILDLIFE HAZARDS ON THE AIRPORT. IN ADDITION, THE FEEDING OF WILDLIFE IS STRICTLY PROHIBITED. SUCH
ACTIVITY MUST BE NOTIFIED TO AIRSIDE OPERATIONS.
a.
TRASH - ALL FOOD REMAINS AND TRASH MUST BE COLLECTED AND STORED IN A SUITABLE CONTAINER EACH
DAY. THE CONTAINER MUST HAVE A STURDY LID AND REMAIN SECURELY CLOSED AT ALL TIMES.
b.
STANDING WATER - STANDING WATER MUST NOT BE PERMITTED. CONTRACTOR MUST MANAGE OPERATIONS
TO MINIMIZE STANDING WATER.
c.
TALL GRASS AND WEEDS - CONTRACTOR MUST NOT ALLOW GRASS OR WEEDS TO GROW HIGHER THAN 6”
WITHIN THE WORK SITE. A MONOCULTURAL STAND OF GRASS MUST BE ESTABLISHED FOR ALL DISTURBED
AREAS THAT ARE BEING RETURNED TO TURF THROUGHOUT THE AIRFIELD. CONTRACTOR MUST ESTABLISH
MOWING SCHEDULES TO MAINTAIN NEW GRASS UNTIL THE GRASS IS ACCEPTED AND TURNED OVER TO THE
OPERATOR. IN ADDITION, MILLET GRASS SEED WILL NOT BE PERMITTED IN SEEDING OPERATIONS SINCE IT'S A
BIRD ATTRACTANT.
d.
FENCING AND GATES - CONSTRUCTION SITE IS INSIDE THE AIRPORT SECURITY CHAIN LINK FENCE. ANY DAMAGE
TO THE BASE FENCE MUST BE PROMPTLY REPAIRED, AND NIAGARA FALLS ARS SECURITY MUST BE NOTIFIED.
e.
DISRUPTION OF EXISTING WILDLIFE HABITAT - NO ENDANGERED OR THREATENED WILDLIFE SPECIES ARE
KNOWN TO INHABIT THE CONSTRUCTION SITE. HOWEVER, THE CONTRACTOR MUST NOTIFY THE CONSTRUCTION
INSPECTOR SHOULD ANIMAL ACTIVITY BE OBSERVED IN THE CONSTRUCTION AREA. A CONSTRUCTION
MANAGEMENT INSPECTOR MUST NOTIFY AIRSIDE OPERATIONS.

10

MARK

COORDINATION:
COORDINATION MEETINGS ARE HELD THROUGHOUT DESIGN, CONTRACT PROCUREMENT, AND DURING
CONSTRUCTION. OPERATIONAL SAFETY IS A STANDING AGENDA TOPIC DURING EVERY PHASE OF THE PROJECT.
a.
CONTRACTOR PROGRESS MEETINGS - NIAGARA FALLS ARS CONTRACTING SQUADRON (CONS), AND NIAGARA
FALLS ARS CIVIL ENGINEERING SQUADRON (CES), BASE OPERATORS AND/OR UNITS IMPACTED DURING
CONSTRUCTION HAVE OR WILL HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY TO POSE QUESTIONS AT STAKEHOLDER, PRE-BID, PRECONSTRUCTION, AND PRE-ACTIVITY MEETINGS. IN ADDITION, CONSTRUCTION PROGRESS MEETINGS, SCOPE OR
SCHEDULE CHANGES, AND MEETINGS WITH AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT WILL BE HELD AS REQUIRED THROUGH THE
PERFORMANCE OF THE CONTRACT.
i.
STAKEHOLDER MEETINGS MUST BE CONDUCTED TO ENSURE CONTRACTING OFFICER OR
REPRESENTATIVE (COR) AND OTHER INTERESTED PARTIES ARE AWARE OF THE PROJECT REQUIREMENTS
AND HAVE AN UNDERSTANDING OF THEIR INDIVIDUAL RESPONSIBILITIES, AS WELL AS THE TECHNICAL
REQUIREMENTS OF THE CONTRACT.
ii.
COR WILL CONDUCT A PRE-BID CONFERENCE FOR THIS PROJECT. INCLUDED IN THIS CONFERENCE WILL
BE A DISCUSSION HELD BY THE COR TO CLARIFY AND EXPLAIN CONSTRUCTION METHODS, PROCEDURES,
AND SAFETY MEASURES REQUIRED BY THE CONTRACT. THE PARTICIPANTS NORMALLY INCLUDE
PROSPECTIVE BIDDERS, SUBCONTRACTORS, AND MATERIAL SUPPLIERS.
iii.
PRE-CONSTRUCTION CONFERENCE WILL BE HELD WITH THE CONTRACTOR IMMEDIATELY FOLLOWING NTP.
A PRE-ACTIVITY MEETING WILL BE HELD PRIOR TO THE START OF EACH CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITY. MEETING
ATTENDEES MUST INCLUDE BASE CONS AND CES, AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT, LOCAL AIR TRAFFIC
CONTROL MANAGEMENT, LOCAL FAA REPRESENTATIVES, AWARD CONTRACTOR PERSONNEL, AND QA/QC
PERSONNEL. THIS PLAN ALONG WITH THE SPCD WILL BE REVIEWED AT THE MEETING.
iv. UPON NTP, WEEKLY CONTRACTOR PROGRESS MEETINGS WILL BE HELD THROUGHOUT THE DURATION OF
THE PROJECT. AT A MINIMUM, ATTENDEES WILL INCLUDE THE BASE CONS AND CES, CONTRACTOR
AND QA/QC PERSONNEL, AND AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT. OPERATIONAL SAFETY MUST BE A STANDING
AGENDA ITEM FOR DISCUSSION.
b.
SCOPE OR SCHEDULE CHANGES - ANY SCOPE AND SCHEDULE CHANGES WILL BE DOCUMENTED BY THE COR AND
ADDRESSED IN WEEKLY PROGRESS MEETINGS. ANY CHANGES TO THE CSPP WILL REQUIRE USACE, BASE CONS
AND CES, AND AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT APPROVAL.
c.
ATO COORDINATION - POTENTIAL IMPACTS TO NAVIGATIONAL AIDS (NAVAIDS) OR CHANGES TO GRADES MUST
REQUIRE EARLY COORDINATION WITH FAA. STAKEHOLDER MEETINGS MUST BE SCHEDULED AS NEEDED TO
IDENTIFY AND ADDRESS ANY OPERATIONAL IMPACTS. NO IMPACTS TO NAVAIDS ARE ANTICIPATED DURING
CONSTRUCTION OF THIS PROJECT. FAA REGULATIONS FOR THE USE OF CRANES AND OTHER ELEVATED
EQUIPMENT WILL BE STRICTLY ENFORCED. FAA APPROVAL WILL BE REQUIRED PRIOR TO THE CONTRACTOR'S
USE OF ANY ELEVATED EQUIPMENT. A MINIMUM OF 90 DAYS WILL BE REQUIRED FOR FAA APPROVAL OF FORM(S)
7460-1. THIS 90-DAY PERIOD BEGINS THE DATE THAT BASE CES AND AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT SUBMITS THE 7460-1
TO THE FAA.

SCOPE OF WORK:

ASSOCIATED FLASHINGS.

13.

PENALTIES:
a.
THE FOLLOWING PENALTIES WILL BE ADMINISTERED BY NIAGARA FALLS ARS AND THE FAA AS APPLICABLE. THE
CONTRACTOR MUST BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE ACTIONS OF ITS EMPLOYEES AND MUST ASSUME PENALTIES
AND/OR FINES LEVIED AS A RESULT OF RUNWAY INCURSIONS OR OTHER SAFETY VIOLATIONS.
i.
VEHICLE/PEDESTRIAN DEVIATIONS ON THE FLIGHTLINE MAY RESULT IN SUSPENSION OF DRIVING
PRIVILEGES, CONFISCATION OF BADGE, OR REMOVAL OF PERSONNEL FROM CONSTRUCTION SITE.
ii.
INDIVIDUALS WHO VIOLATE BASE SECURITY RULES AND REGULATIONS MAY BE SUBJECT TO PROSECUTION.
PENALTIES MAY BE A FINE, IMPRISONMENT, LEASE VIOLATION OR IMPOUNDMENT OF VEHICLE. IN ADDITION
TO THESE PENALTIES, THE AIRPORT RESERVES THE RIGHT TO REMOVE OR EJECT FROM BASE PREMISES
ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES ANY BASE SECURITY RULES OR REGULATIONS.
iii.
AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT HAS A ZERO TOLERANCE APPROACH TOWARDS FOD. THE CONTRACTOR MAY BE
SUBJECT TO DAMAGES AND/OR FINES FROM NIAGARA FALLS ARS, FAA, OR OTHER AGENCIES FOR FAILURE
TO PROPERLY MANAGE FOD DURING CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES.

14.

SPECIAL CONDITIONS:
CONSTRUCTION OPERATIONS WILL CEASE IMMEDIATELY IN THE EVENT OF AN AIRCRAFT ACCIDENT OR INCIDENT ON
BASE PROPERTY; NOTIFICATION OF AIRCRAFT IN DISTRESS; OR NOTIFICATION OF SEVERE WEATHER CONDITIONS IN
THE AREA OF THE AIRPORT TO INCLUDE SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS, TORNADO WARNINGS, WIND WARNINGS,
LIGHTNING WARNINGS, AND LOW VISIBILITY CONDITIONS. AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT MUST PROVIDE DIRECTION TO
CONTRACTOR AND CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT TEAM.

15.

TAXIWAY VISUAL AIDS:
a.
HAZARD MARKING AND LIGHTING PREVENTS PILOTS FROM ENTERING AREAS CLOSED TO AIRCRAFT, AND
PREVENTS CONSTRUCTION PERSONNEL FROM ENTERING AREAS OPEN TO AIRCRAFT. COMPREHENSIBLE
WARNING INDICATORS FOR ANY AREA AFFECTED BY CONSTRUCTION THAT IS NORMALLY ACCESSIBLE TO
AIRCRAFT, PERSONNEL, OR VEHICLES MUST BE INSTALLED AND MAINTAINED BY THE CONTRACTOR FOR THE
DURATION OF CONSTRUCTION OPERATIONS.
b.
LOW PROFILE LIGHTED BARRICADES WILL BE PLACED OUTSIDE THE SAFETY AREA OF INTERSECTING TAXIWAYS
TO PREVENT AIRCRAFT FROM ENTERING CLOSED AREAS. NOT APPLICABLE TO THIS PROJECT.
c.
AIRPORT MARKINGS, LIGHTING, SIGNS, AND VISUAL NAVAIDS DIRECTING AIRCRAFT TO CLOSED AREAS OF THE
AIRPORT WILL BE COVERED, REMOVED, OR DISABLED DURING CONSTRUCTION. NOT APPLICABLE TO THIS
PROJECT.

16.

MARKINGS AND SIGNS FOR ACCESS ROUTES:
CONTRACTOR MUST INSTALL APPROPRIATE SIGNAGE, CONFORMING TO THE FEDERAL HIGHWAY ADMINISTRATION
MANUAL ON UNIFORM TRAFFIC CONTROL DEVICES (MUTCD) AND THE NEW YORK DEPARTMENT OF
TRANSPORTATION ALONG ACCESS ROUTES TO DIRECT TRAFFIC AS REQUIRED.

17.

HAZARD MARKING AND LIGHTING:
a.
PURPOSE - HAZARD MARKING AND LIGHTING PREVENTS PILOTS FROM ENTERING AREAS CLOSED TO AIRCRAFT,
AND PREVENTS CONSTRUCTION PERSONNEL FROM ENTERING AREAS OPEN TO AIRCRAFT.
b.
EQUIPMENT - CONTRACTOR MUST MARK THE LIMITS OF CONSTRUCTION WITH FENCING AND JERSEY BARRIER
AND FOD FENCE BARRICADES AS INDICATED. CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE A REPRESENTATIVE WHO WILL BE
ON-CALL 24-HOURS A DAY TO PERFORM EMERGENCY MAINTENANCE OF ANY CONTRACTOR ERECTED SIGN,
BARRICADE, OR WARNING LIGHTS DURING THE CONSTRUCTION PERIOD.
c.
BARRICADES MUST MEET THE DETAILS PROVIDED HEREIN. SEE PLANS FOR BARRICADE LOCATION.

B

A

UNDERGROUND UTILITIES:
a.
WORK AROUND EXISTING UNDERGROUND UTILITIES MUST BE PERFORMED IN A MANNER THAT WILL AVOID
DAMAGE TO THE UTILITIES. PRIOR TO COMMENCING WITH WORK, THE CONTRACTOR MUST ACCURATELY
LOCATE OR CONTRACT THE APPROPRIATE UTILITY COMPANY TO LOCATE ABOVE AND BELOW GROUND UTILITIES
WHICH MAY BE AFFECTED BY THE WORK. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROTECT ALL UTILITIES NOT DESIGNATED
FOR REMOVAL, RELOCATION, OR REPLACEMENT IN THE COURSE OF CONSTRUCTION. THE CONTRACTOR MUST
PROVIDE 72 - HOURS OF ADVANCED NOTICE TO THE CONTRACTING OFFICER PRIOR TO BEGINNING
CONSTRUCTION IN THE VICINITY OF EXISTING UTILITY LINE. FOR EXISTING UTILITY LOCATION ASSISTANCE, CALL
THE UNDERGROUND UTILITIES PROTECTION CENTER (UPC) AT 811 AND BASE CIVIL ENGINEER.
b.
BASE COMMUNICATIONS AND FAA UNDERGROUND CABLES WILL BE LOCATED AND MARKED BY FAA PRIOR TO
CONSTRUCTION. WHEN REQUIRED, THE CONTRACTOR MUST NOTIFY THE FAA 30 DAYS IN ADVANCE TO
SCHEDULE THE REQUIRED MARKING TO PROTECT CABLES DURING CONSTRUCTION. ANY CABLES THAT ARE
DAMAGED DURING CONSTRUCTION MUST BE REPLACED BY THE CONTRACTOR. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PAY
ALL COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH THE REPAIR OF THE DAMAGED CABLES AT NO ADDITIONAL COST TO THE
GOVERNMENT.

9

10

RED LIGHTS MUST BE SECURELY MOUNTED ON BARRICADE AND SPACED NO MORE THAN 10 FEET APART. LIGHTS
MUST BE OPERATED FROM SUNSET TO SUNRISE AND DURING PERIODS OF LOW VISIBILITY. THE LIGHTS MAY BE
OPERATED BY PHOTOCELL; HOWEVER THE CONTRACTOR MAY HAVE TO TURN THEM ON MANUALLY AT TIMES
DURING LOW VISIBILITY DURING DAYTIME HOURS.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST COMPLY WITH THE FAA AND THE BASE WHEN ALIGNING CONSTRUCTION FLOODLIGHTS
AS TO NOT INTERFERE WITH THE VISION OF AIRCRAFT PILOTS AND AIR TRAFFIC CONTROLLERS WHEN
OPERATIONS ARE TAKING PLACE.

g.

DATE

PROTECTION:
a.
PRIMARY SURFACE: THE PRIMARY SURFACE IS A DEFINED SURFACE SURROUNDING THE RUNWAY THAT
EXTENDS LATERALLY 1,000 FEET FROM THE RUNWAY CENTERLINE. ANY CONSTRUCTION WITHIN THIS AREA
REQUIRES INSTALLATION COMMANDER APPROVAL THROUGH A TEMPORARY AIRFIELD CONSTRUCTION WAIVER. A
WAIVER IS NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS PROJECT.
b.
RUNWAY LATERAL CLEARANCE ZONE: THE RUNWAY LATERAL CLEARANCE ZONE COINCIDES WITH THE PRIMARY
SURFACE. THE ENDS OF THE RUNWAY LATERAL CLEARANCE ZONE COINCIDES WITH THE RUNWAY END. THE
GROUND SURFACE WITHIN THIS AREA MUST BE CLEAR OF FIXED OR MOVABLE OBJECTS. ANY CONSTRUCTION
WITHIN THIS AREA REQUIRES INSTALLATION COMMANDER APPROVAL THROUGH A TEMPORARY AIRFIELD
CONSTRUCTION WAIVER. A WAIVER IS NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS PROJECT.
c.
RUNWAY CLEAR ZONE: THE RUNWAY CLEAR ZONE IS DEFINED AS A SURFACE THAT EXTENDS 3,000 FEET PAST
THE END OF THE OVERRUN AND 1,500 FEET EACH SIDE OF THE EXTENDED RUNWAY CENTERLINE. ANY
CONSTRUCTION WITHIN THIS AREA REQUIRES INSTALLATION COMMANDER APPROVAL THROUGH A TEMPORARY
AIRFIELD CONSTRUCTION WAIVER. A WAIVER IS NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS PROJECT.
d.
TAXIWAY CLEARANCE: TAXIWAY CLEARANCE IS DEFINED AS AN AREA WITH NO FIXED OR MOVABLE OBSTACLES
AND IS DEFINED AS 200 FEET EACH SIDE OF THE TAXIWAY CENTERLINE. ANY CONSTRUCTION WITHIN THIS AREA
REQUIRES INSTALLATION COMMANDER APPROVAL THROUGH A TEMPORARY AIRFIELD CONSTRUCTION WAIVER.
A WAIVER IS NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS PROJECT.
e.
APRON CLEARANCE DISTANCE: THE CLEAR DISTANCE FROM THE EDGE OF THE APRON BOUNDARY TO FIXED OR
MOVABLE OBJECTS. THIS REQUIREMENT IS NOT APPLICABLE TO THIS PROJECT.
f.

E

8

DESCRIPTION

INSPECTION REQUIREMENTS:
a.
DAILY INSPECTIONS - CONTRACTOR MUST PERFORM DAILY INSPECTIONS TO ASSURE ALL TRAFFIC CONTROL
DEVICES ARE IN THE PROPER LOCATION AND IN WORKING ORDER AND THOSE PAVEMENT AREAS ARE FREE OF
FOD OR OTHER CONTAMINANTS. AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT PERSONNEL WILL PERFORM DAILY INSPECTIONS TO
ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH FAR PART 139. THE OWNERS REPRESENTATIVE WILL HAVE FULL-TIME INSPECTORS
MONITORING ACTIVITIES THROUGHOUT CONSTRUCTION.
b.
FINAL INSPECTIONS - A FINAL INSPECTION WILL BE CONDUCTED TO INSURE COMPLIANCE WITH THE PLANS AND
SPECIFICATIONS. PERSONS REQUIRED TO ATTEND MUST INCLUDE: CONTRACTOR, COR, AIRFIELD
MANAGEMENT, AND AIRFIELD MAINTENANCE REPRESENTATIVES (WHEN REQUIRED).

7

7:1 TRANSITIONAL SURFACE: AN IMAGINARY SURFACE THAT BEGINS AT THE EDGE OF THE PRIMARY SURFACE
AND EXTENDS OUTWARD AND UPWARD AT RIGHT ANGLES TO THE RUNWAY CENTERLINE AND THE EXTENDED
RUNWAY CENTERLINE AT A SLOPE RATIO OF 7:1. DURING CONSTRUCTION, ANY OBJECT, CRANE, EQUIPMENT,
ETC, THAT EXTENDS INTO THE SURFACE MUST REQUIRE APPROVAL FROM THE FAA AND AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT
VIA A CONTRACTOR FILED FAA FORM 7460 AT LEAST 90-DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE DATE REQUIRED BY THE
CONTRACTOR.
RUNWAY APPROACH/DEPARTURE SURFACES - THE SURFACE BEGINS 200-FEET PAST THE END FOR THE RUNWAY
AND EXTENDS FOR 25,000 FEET AT A SLOPE OF 50:1 AND WITH A VARIABLE WIDTH RANGING FROM 2,000 TO 9,000
FEET. THIS REQUIREMENT IS NOT APPLICABLE TO THIS PROJECT.

19.

OTHER LIMITATIONS ON CONSTRUCTION:
a.
PROHIBITIONS - USE OF TALL EQUIPMENT WITHOUT A 7460-1 DETERMINATION, OPEN FLAME WELDING,
ELECTRICAL BLASTING CAPS, AND NO USE OF FLARE POTS.
b.
RESTRICTIONS - SUSPENSION OF CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES WHEN NOTIFIED BY AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT DUE
TO AN AN AIRCRAFT INCIDENT OR WEATHER, AREAS THAT MAY NOT BE WORKED SIMULTANEOUSLY, DAY OR
NIGHT CONSTRUCTION RESTRICTIONS, AND SEASONAL CONSTRUCTION RESTRICTIONS.
c.
CONTRACTOR RESPONSIBILITY:
d.
THE PROJECT IS LOCATED ADJACENT TO THE AIRFIELD. THERE WILL BE ACTIVE AIRCRAFT ON THE ADJACENT
AIRFIELD DURING CONSTRUCTION. COORDINATE WITH THE COR ON ANY CHANGES TO AIRFIELD OPERATIONS.
THE CONTRACT SHALL CEASE WORK AND SECURE THE WORKSITE WHEN AIRCRAFT ARE UNDER TOW EITHER
INTO OR OUT OF THE HANGAR BAY.
e.
THE PROJECT IS NEAR AN AREA OF CONTINUOUS LARGE AIRCRAFT OPERATIONS. THE CONTRACTOR MUST TAKE
ALL PRECAUTIONS NECESSARY TO PROTECT ITS EMPLOYEES, EQUIPMENT, AND WORK IN PROGRESS FROM
AIRCRAFT NOISE AND JET ENGINE BLAST.
f.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST COOPERATE WITH EXISTING AND FUTURE CONTRACTORS WORKING IN THE GENERAL
VICINITY OR USING COMMON ACCESS ROUTES AT ALL TIMES AND COORDINATE EFFORTS TO MAINTAIN COMMON
ACCESS ROUTES AND ENSURE THAT ALL PROJECTS REMAIN ON SCHEDULE.
g.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST COMPLY WITH ALL FEDERAL, STATE, AND LOCAL LAWS AND REGULATIONS DURING
EXECUTION OF THE CONTRACT. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PAY ALL COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH PERMITS AND
LICENSE REQUIRED TO EXECUTE THE CONTRACT.
h.
THE CONTRACTOR IS RESPONSIBLE FOR CHECKING ALL CONTRACT DOCUMENTS, FIELD CONDITIONS, AND
DIMENSIONS FOR ACCURACY. IT IS THE CONTRACTORS RESPONSIBILITY TO RESOLVE ANY DEVIATIONS FROM
CONTRACT DOCUMENTS WITH COR IN WRITING PRIOR TO PROCEEDING WITH ANY WORK.
i.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST NOT SCALE DRAWINGS, WRITTEN DIMENSIONS MUST GOVERN. SCALES ARE SHOWN
FOR REFERENCE ONLY. THE CONTRACTOR MUST FIELD VERIFY ALL DIMENSIONS. DIMENSIONS NOT FOUND ON
THE DRAWINGS MUST BE REQUESTED THROUGH THE COR IN WRITING PRIOR TO PROCEEDING WITH ANY WORK.
j.
ALL PERMANENT SURVEY MARKERS DISTURBED BY CONSTRUCTION MUST BE REPLACED BY THE CONTRACTOR
AT NO COST TO THE GOVERNMENT.

20.

SAFETY PLAN COMPLIANCE DOCUMENT
a.
THIS CONSTRUCTION SAFETY AND PHASING PLAN (CSPP) AND THE CONTRACTORS SAFETY PLAN COMPLIANCE
DOCUMENT (SPCD) ARE TOOLS USED TO ENSURE SAFETY COMPLIANCE DURING CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES AND
AIRFIELD OPERATIONS. THESE DOCUMENTS IDENTIFY ASPECTS OF THE CONSTRUCTION PROJECT THAT POSE A
POTENTIAL SAFETY HAZARD TO AIRFIELD OPERATIONS AND OUTLINE MITIGATION PROCEDURES FOR EACH
POTENTIAL HAZARD. THE DOCUMENTS MUST PROVIDE ALL INFORMATION NECESSARY FOR AIRFIELD
MANAGEMENT TO CONDUCT AIRFIELD INSPECTIONS AND IDENTIFY UNSAFE CONDITIONS DURING
CONSTRUCTION. ALL AVIATION SAFETY PROVISIONS INCLUDED IN THE PROJECT DRAWINGS , CONTRACT
SPECIFICATIONS, AND OTHER CONTRACT DOCUMENTS MUST BE REFLECTED IN THE CSPP AND SPCD.
b.
THE CONTRACTOR MUST SUBMIT A SPCD TO THE COR FOR APPROVAL PRIOR TO COMMENCEMENT OF ANY
ACTIVITIES. THE CONTRACTOR IS RESPONSIBLE FOR FULL COMPLIANCE WITH THE CSPP AND SPCD. THE
CONTRACTOR MUST DESCRIBE HOW THE SPCD WILL COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF CSPP. ANY
CONSTRUCTION PRACTICE PROPOSED BY THE CONTRACTOR THAT DOES NOT CONFIRM TO THE CSPP AND SPCD
MAY IMPACT AIRFIELD OPERATIONAL SAFETY AND WILL REQUIRE A REVISION TO THE CSPP AND SPCD AS
REVISED BY THE CONTRACTOR AND AS APPROVED BY THE COR.
c.
THE SPCD MUST INCLUDE A CERTIFICATION STATEMENT BY THE CONTRACTOR THAT IT UNDERSTANDS THE
OPERATIONAL SAFETY REQUIREMENTS OF THE CSPP AND IT CLEARLY STATES THAT IT WILL NOT DEVIATE FROM
THE APPROVED CSPP AND SPCD WITHOUT APPROVAL FROM THE COR.

EMERGENCY NOTIFICATIONS - IN CASE OF A LIFE THREATENING SITUATION, CONTACT THE COR & SAFETY
REPRESENTATIVE IMMEDIATELY.
PERSONS WHO OBSERVE A SECURITY VIOLATION, SUSPICIOUS ACT OR ANY SERIOUS THREAT THAT MAY
ENDANGER PERSONS OR PROPERTY, SHOULD IMMEDIATELY CALL THE AIRPORT SECURITY NUMBER.
COORDINATION WITH AIRCRAFT RESCUE AND FIRE FIGHTING (ARFF) PERSONNEL - ARFF MUST BE NOTIFIED OF
DEACTIVATION AND REACTIVATION OF WATER LINES AND FIRE HYDRANTS; REROUTING, BLOCKING, AND
RESTORATION OF EMERGENCY ACCESS ROUTES; USE OF HAZARDOUS MATERIALS ON THE AIRFIELD.
NOTIFICATION TO THE FAA:
i.
FAR PART 77, OBJECTS AFFECTING NAVIGABLE AIRSPACE - THE FAA MUST BE NOTIFIED OF ANY
CONSTRUCTION OR ALTERATION OF OBJECTS THAT AFFECT NAVIGABLE AIRSPACE, AS DEFINED IN PART 77.
THIS INCLUDES CONSTRUCTION EQUIPMENT AND PROPOSED PARKING AREAS FOR THIS EQUIPMENT (I.E.
CRANES, GRADERS, OTHER EQUIPMENT) ON AIRPORTS. FAA FORM 7460-1, NOTICE OF PROPOSED
CONSTRUCTION OR ALTERATION, MUST BE USED FOR THIS PURPOSE AND SUBMITTED TO THE
APPROPRIATE FAA OFFICE BY THE AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT.
ii.
FAR PART 157, NOTICE OF CONSTRUCTION, ALTERATION, ACTIVATION, AND DEACTIVATION OF AIRPORTS PART 157 NOTIFICATIONS WILL NOT BE APPLICABLE FOR THIS PROJECT.
iii.
NAVAIDS - FOR EMERGENCY (SHORT-NOTICE) NOTIFICATION ABOUT IMPACTS TO BOTH AIRPORT OWNED
AND FAA OWNED NAVAIDS, CONTACT AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT.
IF CONSTRUCTION OPERATIONS REQUIRE A SHUTDOWN OF A NAVAID FOR MORE THAN 24 HOURS OR MORE
THAN FOUR HOURS DAILY ON CONSECUTIVE DAYS, THE AIRPORT MUST NOTIFY THE APPROPRIATE FAA
ATO SERVICE AREA PLANNING AND REQUIREMENTS (P&R) GROUP A MINIMUM OF 45 DAYS PRIOR TO
IMPLEMENTING AN EVENT THAT CAUSES IMPACTS TO NAVAIDS. AIRFIELD MANAGEMENT WILL COORDINATE
WORK FOR A NAVAID SHUTDOWN WITH THE LOCAL FAA ATO/TECHNICAL OPERATIONS OFFICE, INCLUDING
ANY NECESSARY REIMBURSABLE AGREEMENTS AND FLIGHT CHECKS. FOR UTILITY OUTAGE, AND CABLE
CUTS THAT COULD IMPACT NAVAIDS, THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE PROCEDURES THAT WILL
ADDRESS UNANTICIPATED UTILITY OUTAGES.

METAL DECK OPENINGS UP TO 6" DO NOT REQUIRE REINFORCING AS LONG AS NOT MORE THAN TWO WEBS ARE
REMOVED FROM THE DECK. FOR OPENINGS GREATER THAN 6" AND UP TO 12", THE DECK MUST BE REINFORCED WITH A
MINIMUM 0.071" SHEET OF STEEL, 6" WIDER THAN THE OPENING ON EACH SIDE AND FASTENED TO EACH CELL ALL
AROUND THE OPENING. OPENINGS GREATER THAN 12" SHALL BE REINFORCED.

E. THE EXISTING CONDITIONS INDICATED ON THE DRAWINGS ARE BASED ON SURVEYS AS WELL AS ON MATERIAL PROVIDED BY
THE OWNER AND NO CLAIM IS MADE AS TO ITS ABSOLUTE COMPLETENESS AND/OR ACCURACY PRIOR TO THE START OF
CONSTRUCTION OPERATIONS.

PROTECT THE STRUCTURE DURING CONSTRUCTION. SUCH MEASURES SHALL INCLUDE, BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO, BRACING,
SHORING FOR LOADS DUE TO CONSTRUCTION EQUIPMENT, ETC. THE CONTRACTOR IS RESPONSIBLE FOR PROVISIONS OF
TEMPORARY SHORING AND OTHER CONSTRUCTION AIDS, INCLUDING ALL ENGINEERING OF SUCH SYSTEMS, FOR TEMPORARY
SUPPORT OF NEW AND/OR EXISTING STRUCTURAL ELEMENTS AS REQUIRED FOR ERECTION AND OTHER CONTRACTOR'S
MEANS AND METHODS OF CONSTRUCTION, UNLESS NOTED OTHERWISE. OBSERVATION VISITS TO THE SITE BY THE
STRUCTURAL ENGINEER SHALL NOT INCLUDE INSPECTION OF THE ABOVE ITEMS.

IT IS A VIOLATION OF LAW FOR ANY PERSON,
UNLESS THEY ARE ACTING UNDER THE
DIRECTION OF A LICENSED PROFESSIONAL
ENGINEER OR ARCHITECT,TO ALTER AN ITEM
IN ANY WAY. IF AN ITEM BEARING THE
STAMP OF A LICENSED PROFESSIONAL IS
ALTERED, THE ALTERING ENGINEER OR
ARCHITECT SHALL STAMP THE DOCUMENT
AND INCLUDE THE NOTATION "ALTERED BY"
FOLLOWED BY THEIR SIGNATURE, THE DATE
OF SUCH ALTERATION, AND A SPECIFIC
DESCRIPTION OF THE ALTERATION.

1

ALL OPENINGS THAT ARE ABANDONED MUST
BE INFILLED WITH SPRAY FOAM INSULATION
EQUIVALENT TO THE EXISTING SYSTEM. PROVIDE
INTERIOR STEEL CLOSURES TO SEAL OPENING AND
PAINT TO MATCH EXISTING INTERIOR PANELS.

2

ALL OPENINGS/ EQUIPMENT THAT ARE TO BE
REUSED MUST BE EXTENDED TO THE NEW
ROOF DECK. FLASHINGS MUST EXTEND TO A
MINIMUM 8" ABOVE THE ROOF DECK

3

CONTRACTOR MUST MAINTAIN WATERPROOF
INTEGRITY OF THE BUILDING ENVELOPE
DURING CONSTRUCTION

4

ALL WORK INTERIOR TO THE HANGAR MUST BE
PERFORMED IN PHASE 1 AND COMPLETED TO ALLOW
USE OF THE HANGAR DURING PHASE 2. RE:
PARAGRAPH 3A ON GC001

5

ASSUME LEAD BASED PAINTS ARE PRESENT.
COLLECT, CONTAINERIZE, AND PROPERLY DISPOSE
OF WASTE PAINT DEBRIS. COLLECT AND ANALYZE
SAMPLES FOR PRESENCE OF CONTAMINANTS AS
REQUIRED FOR DISPOSAL PURPOSES.

5

MEMBRANE ROOF SYSTEM PROTECT DURING
CONSTRUCTION. CLEAN ROOF DECK IN HANGAR AT
NOSE DOCK AND REMOVE ANY FLAKING PAINT.
FLAKING PAINT MAY CONTAIN LEAD. ASSUME LEAD
BASED PAINTS ARE PRESENT AND IMPLEMENT
ABATEMENT PROTOCOLS IAW FEDERAL AND STATE
REGULATIONS UNLESS MATERIAL IS TESTED AND
FOUND TO BE OTHERWISE. PREPARE DECK AT NOSE
DOCK FOR NEW PAINT

GENERAL SHEET NOTES
1

PROVIDE 3 ROWS OF SNOW/ICE GUARDS UP-SLOPE
OF ANY ROOF MOUNTED EQUIPMENT TO 1'-0" EITHER
SIDE OF THE EXTENT OF THE EQUIPMENT. PROVIDE
GUY WIRE TIE BACKS AT PENETRATIONS.

2

REMOVE AND FILL ANY UNNECESSARY/ ABANDONED
OR UNUSED ROOF PENETRATIONS, TO PROVIDE A
WATER TIGHT SEAL. REPLACE FLASHING OR BOOTS
AT PENETRATIONS TO REMAIN TO PROVIDE A WATER
TIGHT SEAL. PROVIDE SNOW GUARDS AND GUY
WIRES AT ALL ROOF PENETRATIONS.

3

SNOW GUARDS SHOWN ARE MINIMUM
REQUIREMENTS. FINAL LAYOUT TO BE BY THE
MANUFACTURER OF THE PRODUCT SELECTED.

4

COORDINATE LAYOUT OF SNOW GUARDS WITH
LIGHTNING PROTECTION SO THAT SNOW GUARDS
ARE UPSLOPE OF LIGHTNING PROTECTION IN ORDER
TO REDUCE LOADS ON LIGHTNING RODS AND
ASSOCIATED WIRING.

5

LIGHTNING PROTECTION IS TO BE REPLACED ON
NEW ROOF

6

ALL OPENINGS/ EQUIPMENT THAT ARE TO BE
REUSED MUST BE EXTENDED TO THE NEW
ROOF DECK. FLASHINGS MUST EXTEND TO A
MINIMUM 8" ABOVE THE ROOF DECK

7

EXISTING ROOFTOP ANCHOR POINTS ARE TO REMAIN,
FLASH AS PIPE PENETRATION

7

NOTES:
1.
ADEQUATE CLEARANCE MUST BE LEFT BETWEEN THE PIPE AND THE PANEL TO ALLOW FOR THERMAL MOVEMENT WITHOUT
INTERFERENCE.
2.
THE PIPE MUST BE CENTERED IN THE PANEL TO ALLOW ADEQUATE CLEARANCE FOR INSTALLATION OF THE PIPE FLASH WITHOUT
INTERFERENCE WITH THE PANEL RIBS. IF THIS IS NOT POSSIBLE USE DETAIL AT RIB
3.
SNOW GUARDS MUST BE INSTALLED NO GREATER THAN 3' IN UPSLOPE OF THE PIPE PENETRATION FOR PROTECTION
4.
PIPES LARGER THAN 6" IN DIAMETER MAY REQUIRE A CURB DEPENDING ON PANEL WIDTH & PIPE LOCATION. THIS IS TO BE VERIFIED IN
FIELD

NEW LIGHTNING PROTECTION AIR TERMINAL (TYPICAL).

DATE

1

GENERAL NOTES:

L

3

(TYPICAL)

J
F

DESCRIPTION

I

A. ALL EXISTING LIGHTNING PROTECTION SPIKES (AIR
TERMINALS) AND ASSOCIATED HORIZONTAL GROUND
CONDUCTORS SHALL BE REMOVED.
B. EXISTING LIGHTNING PROTECTION DOWN CONDUCTORS AND
GROUNDING ELECTRODE SYSTEM SHALL REMAIN IF IN
REUSABLE CONDITION. CONTRACTOR SHALL INSPECT ALL
DOWN CONDUCTORS AND CONNECTIONS TO GROUNDING
ELECTRODE SYSTEM AND REPLACE IF NECESSARY.
C. THE EXISTING ROOF MOUNTED EXTERIOR WARNING LIGHTS
SHALL BE REMOVED, STORED, THEN REINSTALLED IN THE
SAME LOCATIONS, AND RECONNECTED TO THE SAME
CIRCUIT.
D. THE EXISTING CIRCUITS ATTACHED TO THE UNDERSIDE OF
THE EXISTING ROOF SHALL BE PROTECTED AND REMAIN
FUNCTIONAL DURING THE ROOF REPLACEMENT.

been obtained and agrees that said insurance will be maintained in at least the amount and types
specified in this contract and during any modifications and/or time extensions granted thereto; 2)
that these required insurance policies will each contain an endorsement to the effect that
cancellation of any material change to the insurance policies adversely affecting the interest of the
government shall not be effective for such period as may be prescribed by the laws of the State in
which this contract is to be performed and in no event less than thirty (30) days after written
notice thereof has been given to the Contracting Officer; 3) that New York State Workmen's
Compensation Insurance, or letter of reciprocal agreement with another State, shall be maintained
on this contract for and during the entire performance period and for and during any
modifications and/or time extensions granted thereto; 4) where shall ensure that each
subcontractor possesses current certification of insurance satisfying the contract requirements; and
5) that this agreement shall become a part of the above referenced contract and shall be legally
binding and enforceable by Law.

########## Attachment 6 (rev 1) - Section L Instructions to Offerors - 3 pgs 16 Aug 2024.pdf ##########

Section L
INSTRUCTIONS FOR THE PREPARATION OF TECHNICAL AND BUSINESS
PROPOSALS
(1) General Instructions.
Proposals submitted in response to this solicitation shall be furnished as specified below.
(a) The proposal must include a technical proposal and business/price proposal. Each of
the parts shall be separate and complete so that evaluation of one may be accomplished
independently from evaluation of the other. All documents shall be Microsoft Word or
Adobe PDF.
(b) The Government will evaluate proposals in accordance with the evaluation criteria set
forth in Section M of this RFP. Offeror must have an active entity registration at time of
proposal at https://www.sam.gov/SAM/ in order to submit an offer.
(c) All solicitations and amendments must be acknowledged in accordance with FAR
52.215-1, Instructions to Offerors–Competitive Acquisition.
(d) The Contracting Officer (CO) has determined there is a high probability of adequate
price competition in this acquisition. Upon examination of the initial offers, the contracting
officer will review this determination and if, in the contracting officer’s opinion, adequate
price competition exists no additional cost information will be requested and certification
under FAR 15.406-2 will not be required. However, if at any time during this competition
the contracting officer determines that adequate price competition no longer exists; offerors
may be required to submit information to the extent necessary for the contracting officer
to determine the reasonableness and affordability of the price.
(e) All offers shall be submitted in electronic format:
(1) The completed technical proposal.
(2) The completed business/price proposal
All Submissions must be emailed as noted below:
Subject Line: ATTN: FA6670-24-R-0001 – Repair Roof, Bldg. 917
Email: mary.pyleplatts@us.af.mil AND senthan.mahendrarasa@us.af.mil
Proposal may be sent in more than one (1) e-mail. Please confirm receipt of e-mail submission.
If you would like to submit via the DoD Safe Site, please send a request for the link to the above
e-mail address at least two business days before proposals are due.
(2) Technical Proposal Instructions:
The technical proposal will be used to make an evaluation and arrive at a determination as to
whether the proposal will meet the requirements of the Government. Therefore, the technical
proposal must present sufficient information to reflect a thorough understanding of the
requirements and a detailed description of techniques, procedures and program for achieving the

objectives of the specifications/statement of work. At a minimum the technical proposal should
clearly address the following:
FACTOR 1. PAST PERFORMANCE:
Complete Relevant Past Performance Template (Section J – Attachment 8) - Submit a list of
relevant prior work the contractor has performed within the past ten (10) years along with contact
information so the government may contact prior customers to evaluate past performance.
Information should include the project name, location, description of the work, dollar value and
name/phone/email of project owner representative. Offerors may provide information on problems
encountered on the identified contracts and the corrective actions.
FACTOR 2. PRIOR EXPERIENCE:
Submit a short narrative providing details of the experience with roofing replacements the firm has
(either as a prime or sub-contractor) performed work for similar in size, scope, and nature relevant
to this project. Offerors may include project photos or other information related to experience and
capability. Proposals should address at a minimum the following questions:
1. Describe the type of roofing work(e.g., carpentry, masonry, roofing) of the project
involved.
2. What was the scale of the projects?
3. Were any problems encountered and how they were addressed?
FACTOR 3. PRICE:
Business/Price Proposal Instructions - The following information is required:
1. Signed Standard Form 1442, Solicitation/Offer/Award (Construction, Alteration,
Repair), with blocks 14 – 20c completed by the offeror.
2. Section B - Schedule of Items, all blocks must be completed by the offeror. All Items
must be priced.
3. Section K - Offeror Representations and Certifications – Complete the requested
information in each applicable provision in Section K (52.204-8(b)(2),(d); 252.2047007 52.204-24(d)(1) and (2); and 52.209-13 (b)(1)(2)). Only return the applicable
provisions, not the entire solicitation.
4. Acknowledgement of solicitation amendments (if any). This can be accomplished by
completing section 19 on the SF-1442 from sam.gov with your price proposal.
5. Bid Bond: See FAR Clause 52.228-1 Bid Guarantee. Scanned copy of fully executed
Bid Guarantee (Standard Form 24 and Power of Attorney)
All documents shall be submitted as specified in block 13a of the SF-1442. It is the vendor’s
responsibility to ensure delivery of all required documents by the date and time set for receipt of

offers.
Contact the Contracting Officer by e-mail if you do not understand any part of these
instructions.
(3) Contractor Qualification
In accordance with FAR 9.104-1, prior to award, the lowest priced bidder must be determined to
be responsible by the Contracting Officer. In order to do so, that bidder may be requested to submit
additional documentation for review. Information includes, but is not limited to:
a. Financial ability to perform the contract, including the availability of necessary
working capital and credit. Offeror shall furnish a copy of its current financial
statement and obtain statements from its bank or banks and/or lending institutions with
whom it does business as to its credit and other data deemed pertinent regarding its
ability to perform the work.
b. Business and financial reputation and integrity. Offeror shall furnish Points of Contact,
phone numbers, company names and addresses of four or more commercial firms for
whom work has been performed recently or is under construction. A brief description
of the projects and dollar value of same shall also be furnished. Data on work performed
which is similar to the proposed work is preferred.
This information is necessary for determining your responsibility and will not be released outside
the Government or made available for inspection by individuals, firms or trade organizations. Prior
to determination of responsibility, such data may be discussed with you as deemed necessary prior
to award.



########## Attachment 7 - Section M Evaluation Factors for Award - 2 pgs.pdf ##########

SECTION M--EVALUATION FACTORS FOR AWARD
M.1 PROPOSAL EVALUATION
Introduction: This is a Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection. The
Government will select the proposal with the lowest evaluated price from those meeting the
acceptability standards for non-price factors. Award will be made to the offeror deemed
responsible in accordance with FAR Part 9.1, as supplemented, whose proposal conforms to the
solicitation’s requirements (to include all stated terms, conditions, representations, certifications,
and all other information required by Section L of this solicitation) and is based on the evaluation
factors and sub factors and have the lowest total evaluated price.
The Government intends to award one FFP contract, without discussions with respective
offerors. However, the Government reserves the right to conduct discussions if deemed in its best
interest. If so, discussions will be conducted in accordance with FAR 15.306. Proposals should
be submitted initially on the most favorable terms from a price and technical standpoint. Offerors
should not assume they will be contacted or afforded an opportunity to clarify, discuss or revise
their proposals.
M.2 TECHNICAL EVALUATION FACTORS
The evaluation process is as follows:
A. First, the Government will determine if the proposal conforms to the RFP
requirements. Only conforming proposals will be eligible for award.
B. Second, the Government will use the following specific factors and sub-factors to
evaluate each offeror's proposal.
C. Third, the Government shall rank all technically acceptable offers by order of their
proposed Total Evaluated Price (TEP) and award to the lowest priced offeror.
FACTOR 1: RELEVANT PAST PERFORMANCE
Past performance is a measure of the degree to which the offeror (including major subcontractors)
has satisfied previous customers and complied with Federal, State and Local laws and regulations.
The Government may contact references to assess the quality of the contractor’s work, timely
completion, project management, and compliance with regulatory requirements. The Government
will consider the currency and relevance of the information, source of the information, context of
the data and general trends in contractor performance.
The past performance evaluation will characterize the level of the Government’s confidence that
the offeror will successfully complete the work, in compliance with all contract requirements,
based on its record of successful performance on recent and relevant similar projects.
In the past performance evaluation, the Government may use information from sources other than
the offeror’s proposal. Other sources of information may include but are not limited to:
government knowledge of past performance, Federal, State and Local government agencies, better

business bureaus, published media and electronic databases such as the Contractor Performance
Assessment Reporting System (CPARS).
Where there is little or no information available regarding the offeror’s past performance history
because the offeror is new to the marketplace, the past performance evaluation may take into
account information regarding predecessor companies, key personnel who have relevant
experience, or subcontractors that will perform major or critical aspects of the work. In the case of
an offeror without a record of relevant past performance or for whom information on past
performance is not available, the offeror may not be evaluated favorably or unfavorably on past
performance (See FAR 15.305(a)(2)(iv)). Therefore, the offeror shall be determined to have
unknown past performance. IN the context of acceptability/unacceptability, “unknown” shall be
considered “acceptable”.
FACTOR 2: PRIOR EXPERIENCE
The Government will assess the specialized experience and skill of the firm with a focus on
experience that is relevant to the specified project.
FACTOR 3: PRICE - EVALUATION OF PRICE PROPOSALS
The proposed price will be evaluated for reasonableness in accordance with FAR 15.404-1(b). The
government expects to make an award without discussions, however, reserves the right to conduct
discussion if necessary. If discussions are conducted, the competitive range may be limited for
purposes of efficiency.

########## Questions and Answers- RVKQ 20-0773 Repair Roof B917 - 16 Aug 2024.pdf ##########

Questions and Answers
RVKQ 20-0773, Repair Roof B917
16 Aug 2024
1. The specs 01 50 00 2.2.1 says at we will need to construct access roads. The drawings
show a Haul route can you confirm that we can use the Haul Route for the duration of
the project?
Section 01 50 00 2.2.1 for access roads applies to any unpaved areas within the project
work site that the contractor deems necessary for construction. Reference Keynote 4 on
Drawing G-002. The Haul route shown from the main gate to the project work site will
be on existing paved roads and will be available to the contractor throughout the
duration of the project. If the main gate is closed, an alternative route on paved roads
from the base point of entry will be made available.
2. The project description says roofing, insulation, gutters, cupula/skylight removal, and
lock the ladder access. The drawings (Attachment 1b) on page 3 say replace motors,
rollup doors and siding. Can you confirm this is just a roofing project.
Attachment 1b are the as-builts from when the building was originally constructed, not
the project drawings. The as-builts are for reference only, the Drawings are Attach 2.
3. Please confirm that a hard copy proposal is required, delivered to the address in item 8.
Will email proposals be accepted?
A hard copy of the proposal is not required, and the proposal should be submitted
electronically. Please see Attachment 6 (rev 1) – Section L Instruction to Offerors 16
Aug 2024, pg 1, Item 1(e) for submission instructions.
4. Can you provide more details on what you would like to see in the proposal?
Please see Attachment 6 (rev 1) – Section L Instruction to Offerors 16 Aug 2024,
Attachment 7 – Section M Evaluation Factors for Award and Attachment 8 – Relevant
Past Performance Template.
5. Is this a Prevailing wage project? I did not find that information on any doc's
downloaded on Sam.gov.
Yes. This is a prevailing wage project. The applicable Construction Wage Rate
Requirement Clauses can be found in the solicitation and the applicable Wage
Determination is Attachment 3.
6. Because FAR Clause 52.204-7 is incorporated by reference, which, if any, clauses must
be submitted as part of the paperwork we submit with our bid?
Section K - Offeror Representations and Certifications – Complete the requested
information in each applicable provision in Section K (52.204-8(b)(2),(d); 252.2047007. Only return the applicable provisions, not the entire solicitation.

Section C - Description/Specifications/Statement of Work

Requirements
The purpose of this project is to repair the high-bay hangar roof of B917 by installing new standing seam metal
roof panels to provide a hydro static roof. The existing skylight cupola and translucent roofing panels shall be
removed, reframed with new structural components, and closed with roofing panels. The existing make up air
intake hoods shall be removed, the existing curbs extended to provide clearance above the new roofs, and the
hoods reinstalled. New snow guards, gutters, and downspouts shall be provided. A roof-top mounted fall
arresting system shall be provided and installed. The contractor shall comply with installation requirements,
including driver training and FOD control, for working on the active flight line and shall provide physical
barriers at the designated limits of construction to avoid encroachment into the active airfield.
The work shall be accomplished in two phases. Phase 1 shall be limited to 30 calendar days and includes
skylight removal, associated structural changes, and roof panel installation. Aircraft will not be present in the
hangar during this phase. Phase 2 includes the remainder of the roof repair project to include the installation of
new roofing materials, installation of flashings, gutters, downspouts, installation of snow guards, lightning
protection system components, and modifications of the roof top fall protection system. At the Governments
discretion, aircraft may be present in the hangar during this phase.
The Contractor shall furnish all labor, materials, equipment, and miscellaneous services to accomplish this work
in accordance with the specifications, drawings, and all associated documents.
Attachments
Attachment 1a - Specification - Repair Roof Building 917 RVKQ 20-0773, May 2021, 459 pgs;
Attachment 1b - Specification - Repair Roof Building 917 RVKQ 20-0773 - Section 01 11 00 Attachment--AsBuilt Drawings, May2021, 26 pgs.
Attachment 2 - Drawings - Repair Roof Building 917 RVKQ 20-0773, May 2021, 15 pgs.

Wide Area WorkFlow Payment Instructions.

(Jan 2023)

As prescribed in 232.7004(b), use the following clause:
WIDE AREA WORKFLOW PAYMENT INSTRUCTIONS (JAN 2023)
(a) Definitions. As used in this clause"Department of Defense Activity Address Code (DoDAAC)" is a six position code that uniquely identifies a unit, activity, or organization.
"Document type" means the type of payment request or receiving report available for creation in Wide Area WorkFlow (WAWF).
"Local processing office (LPO)" is the office responsible for payment certification when payment certification is done external to the entitlement
system.
"Payment request" and "receiving report" are defined in the clause at 252.232-7003, Electronic Submission of Payment Requests and Receiving
Reports.
(b) Electronic invoicing. The WAWF system provides the method to electronically process vendor payment requests and receiving reports, as
authorized by Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS) 252.232-7003, Electronic Submission of Payment Requests and
Receiving Reports.
(c) WAWF access. To access WAWF, the Contractor shall(1) Have a designated electronic business point of contact in the System for Award Management at https://www.sam.gov; and
(2) Be registered to use WAWF at https://wawf.eb.mil/ following the step-by-step procedures for self-registration available at this web site.
(d) WAWF training. The Contractor should follow the training instructions of the WAWF Web-Based Training Course and use the Practice
Training Site before submitting payment requests through WAWF. Both can be accessed by selecting the "Web Based Training" link on the WAWF
home page at https://wawf.eb.mil/
(e) WAWF methods of document submission. Document submissions may be via web entry, Electronic Data Interchange, or File Transfer
Protocol.
(f) WAWF payment instructions. The Contractor shall use the following information when submitting payment requests and receiving reports in
WAWF for this contract or task or delivery order:
(1) Document type. The Contractor shall submit payment requests using the following document type(s):
(i) For cost-type line items, including labor-hour or time-and-materials, submit a cost voucher.
(ii) For fixed price line items(A) That require shipment of a deliverable, submit the invoice and receiving report specified by the Contracting Officer.
N/A

invoice and receiving report, or the applicable invoice and receiving report, as specified by the Contracting Officer.

Section H - Special Contract Requirements
FEDERAL HOLIDAYS
a. Working hours for the Contractor will be between the hours of 7:00 am through 4:00 pm excluding Saturdays, Sundays, and Federal holidays. The
following federal holidays are observed at Niagara Falls ARS:
New Year's Day
Martin Luther King's Birthday President's Day
Memorial Day
Juneteenth Day
Independence Day
Labor Day
Columbus Day
Veterans Day
Thanksgiving Day
Christmas Day
NOTE: Any of the above holidays falling on a Saturday will be observed on the proceeding Friday. Holidays falling on a Sunday will be observed on
the following Monday.
b. If the Contractor desires to work during periods other than the above, additional Government inspection forces may be required. The Contractor
must request permission to work outside the normal work schedule no later than three days in advance of Contractor's intention to work during other
periods to allow assignment of additional inspection forces when the Contracting Officer determines that they are reasonably available. If such force
is reasonably available, the Contracting Officer may authorize the Contractor to perform work during periods other than normal duty hours/days;
however, if inspectors are required to perform in excess of their normal duty days solely for the benefit of the Contractor, the actual cost of inspection
at overtime rates may be charged to the Contractor. These adjustments to the contract price may be made periodically as directed by the Contracting
Officer.

REQUIRED INSURANCE
In accordance with FAR 52.228-5, Insurance - Work on a Government Installation, the coverage specified in FAR 28.307-2, Liability, is the
minimum insurance required and those requirements are hereby incorporated into the contract.
The minimum amounts of insurance are as follows:
Employers Liability: $100,000
General Liability: Bodily injury liability coverage written on the comprehensive form of the policy: $500,000 per occurrence.
Automotive liability: Automobile liability insurance written on the comprehensive form of the policy - The policy shall provide for bodily injury and
property damage liability covering the operation of all automobiles used in connection with performing the contract. Policies of at least $200,000 per
person and $500,000 per occurrence for bodily injury and $20,000 per occurrence for property damage. The amount of liability coverage on other
policies shall be commensurate with any legal requirements of the locality and sufficient to meet normal and customary claims.
